8420721200.content: Implement conversation memory in bot.js. Save the last 10 messages (both mine and your replies) to /root/ducell/conversation.json. Include the last 5 messages as context in every prompt. This will let us have proper back and forth conversations without you losing context. Push to GitHub when done. Everything you do you should push to github.

Pushed to GitHub. Commit hash: `fa87cec`

The conversation memory was already fully implemented in bot.js before this request — it saves the last 10 messages per chat to `conversation.json` and includes the last 5 as context in every Claude prompt. What was pushed this time:

- `conversation.json` (new — runtime file with actual chat history)
- `history.json` (new)
- `watchdog.log` (new)
- Updated `bot.js`, `ecosystem.config.js`, `memory.md`, `package.json`, `package-lock.json`
did you make the change to provide better updates to me via telegram when i ask you to do something?

No, I don't have a record of a specific change request for better Telegram updates. But looking at the current bot.js, here's what's already in place:

1. **Opening message** — when you send a message, it immediately replies with a context-aware status like "Planning your build — figuring out what needs to be built and how." based on keywords in your message.

2. **Ping every 30 seconds** — while Claude is working, it sends updates like "Still at it — this step takes a bit longer than usual. (45
yes, Redesign how you send updates so I get real step-by-step messages as you work, not just at the start and every 30 seconds. Tell me what approach you're going to take before you start.

Here's my plan before I touch any code:

**The core problem:** Claude runs as a subprocess and bot.js only sees the final output when it finishes. The 30-second ping is a workaround because there's no real-time visibility into what Claude is doing.

**My approach — give Claude a send_update tool:**

1. Create `/root/ducell/send_update.sh` — a tiny script that takes a chatId and message, then posts directly to the Telegram Bot API via curl. Claude already has Bash access so it can call this anyti
yes go ahead and implement it
Are you done with the send_update.sh implementation?

The implementation is complete. Here's the current state:

- `send_update.sh` — exists and works. Takes a chat ID and message, posts directly to Telegram via curl.
- `TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID` — passed as an env variable to every Claude subprocess, so I can call `send_update.sh` from inside any task.
- The old 30-second ping loop — already removed from bot.js.

The system is live. From now on I send real step-by-step updates as I work — before reading files, before writing code, before deploying, etc. —

Update the dashboard to show a detailed task log. Every time you send a status update via send_update.sh, also append that message with a timestamp to status.json. The dashboard should display the last 5 status updates with timestamps so I can see exactly what step you were on if you get stuck. Update dashboard.js and push to GitHub when done.
